page.title: peripheral arterial disease — Disease Intelligence | OSMF
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for peripheral arterial disease: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for peripheral arterial disease: 130 alterations and 292 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Association between alcohol consumption and peripheral artery disease.
A combination of GLA and EPA may have therapeutic value in peripheral arterial disease.
A high-score Mediterranean dietary pattern is associated with a reduced risk of peripheral arterial disease in Italian patients with Type 2 diabetes.
A higher intake of vegetable lipids, Vitamin E and higher concentrations of serum HDL cholesterol may reduce the risk of peripheral arterial disease.
A proprietary ginkgo biloba extract is safe and effective in the treatment of patients with intermittent claudication. - Article 2
A Tibetan herbal formula, Padma 28, provides significant relief from Peripheral Arterial Occlusive Disease-related symptoms
Alcohol consumption and cardiovascular disease.
Alpha Lipoic Acid confers pain relief in patients with Peripheral Arterial Disease.
Annurca apple polyphenolic extract could be a promising natural and safe tool for treatment of symptoms related to peripheral arterial disease.
Cocoa flavanols in peripheral artery disease.
Combined supplementation with vitamin C and vitamin E may be of clinical benefit in improving the cutaneous microcirculation by reducing oxidative stress in patients with peripheral arterial disease.
Connective tissue massage improves blood circulation in the lower limbs of type 2 diabetic patients and may be useful to slow the progression of Peripheral Arterial Disease.
Fish oil has a beneficial effect on blood viscosity in peripheral vascular disease.
Fish oil supplementation improves walking distaince in peripheral arterial disease.
Folate reduces plasma homocysteine and improves arterial function in patients with peripheral arterial disease.
Greater frequency of fruit and vegetable consumption is associated with lower prevalence of peripheral artery disease.
Higher consumption of folate may contribute to the prevention of peripheral arterial disease.

Higher consumption of specific nutrients, including antioxidants (vitamin A, C, and E), vitamin B(6), fiber, folate, and omega-3 fatty acids have a significant protective effect against peripheral arterial disease.
Hyperbaric oxygen therapy augmented circulating levels of EPCs and angiogenesis factors, and improved the blood flow in the ischemic area.
Hypovitaminosis D is associated with subclinical peripheral arterial disease, independently of other cardiovascular risk factors.
L-arginine improves symptoms of intermittent claudication in patients with arterial occlusive disease.
L-arginine infusion decreases peripheral arterial resistance and inhibits platelet aggregation in healthy subjects.
Low serum 25(OH)D levels were significantly associated with a higher prevalence of PAD in type 2 diabetes patients 65 years of age.
Low serum vitamin C correlates with an increased risk of peripheral arterial disease in current smokers.
Milled flaxseed lowers total and LDL cholesterol in patients with peripheral artery disease.
Omega-3 fatty acids improve endothelial function in peripheral arterial disease.
Organochlorine pesticides are a potent risk factor for peripheral arterial diseases in obese persons.
Propionyl-L-carnitine improves arterial dilatation via NOX2 inhibition in patients with peripheral artery disease.
Propionyl-L-carnitine imrpoves exercise performance in peripheral arterial disease.
Pycnogenol reduces cramps and muscular pain common in diabetics and claudicants.
Serum vitamin D and diabetic foot complications.
Supplementation with orange and blackcurrant juice improves inflammatory markers in patients with peripheral arterial disease.
The effects of dietary flaxseed on cardiac arrhythmias and claudication in patients with peripheral arterial disease.
The omega-3 fatty acid docosapentaenoic acid reduces the risk of peripheral arterial disease associated with smoking.
The results showed that bicycling might be as useful as walking in patients with peripheral artery disease.
These preliminary results suggest a therapeutic effect of cocoa on walking performance in people with peripheral artery disease.
These results suggest an inverse association between cereal fiber intake and peripheral arterial disease risk in men.
Vitamin D deficiency is associated with an increased amputation risk in veterans with peripheral artery disease.
Vitamin D status is associated the risk of peripheral arterial disease, especially in African-Americans.
Vitamin E intake is inversely associated with peripheral arterial disease.
